title: styphelia viridis subsp. breviflora
intro: Styphelia viridis subsp. breviflora is a subspecies of the Styphelia viridis plant, a flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to Australia and is also known as the 'Green Styphelia' or 'Dwarf Green Styphelia'. The medicinal properties of Styphelia viridis subsp. breviflora include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. Styphelia viridis subsp. breviflora is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support respiratory health.
